final_output.final_deliverable:
## 10 links collected from Wikipedia’s Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page)

Evidence that these links are on the Main Page: the Main Page visit output includes them in its extracted link list, e.g. it lists: `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents`, `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events`, `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random`, etc. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page). The visible page text also shows navigation items like “Other areas of Wikipedia” and “Wikipedia’s sister projects”, confirming these are Main Page navigation links (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

> Main Page text examples showing these navigation areas include: “**Other areas of Wikipedia**” and “**Wikipedia's sister projects**” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 1) Contents
- **Link text/title:** Contents
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
- **Brief description:** The Wikipedia “Contents” page is a navigation hub that helps readers browse the encyclopedia by topic and category.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 2) Current events
- **Link text/title:** Current events
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events
- **Brief description:** The Current events portal aggregates and organizes ongoing and recent news events, typically by date and topic.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 3) Random article
- **Link text/title:** Random article
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
- **Brief description:** A special page that takes you to a random Wikipedia article each time you open it.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 4) About Wikipedia
- **Link text/title:** About Wikipedia
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About
- **Brief description:** An overview page describing what Wikipedia is, how it works, and its goals and practices.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 5) Contact Wikipedia
- **Link text/title:** Contact Wikipedia
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us
- **Brief description:** Provides ways to contact Wikipedia/Wikimedia (e.g., help contacts and organizational contact paths).
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 6) Help
- **Link text/title:** Help
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents
- **Brief description:** The main help portal for using Wikipedia, offering guidance and links to help topics.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 7) Learn to edit
- **Link text/title:** Learn to edit
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction
- **Brief description:** An introductory help page for newcomers that explains basic Wikipedia editing.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 8) Community portal
- **Link text/title:** Community portal
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Community_portal
- **Brief description:** A central hub for Wikipedia editors with links to collaboration spaces, tasks, and community resources.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page explicitly describes this area: “**Community portal – The central hub for editors, with resources, links, tasks, and announcements.**” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and the extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Community_portal` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 9) Recent changes
- **Link text/title:** Recent changes
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:RecentChanges
- **Brief description:** A live feed of the most recent edits across Wikipedia.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:RecentChanges` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

### 10) Upload file
- **Link text/title:** Upload file
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:File_upload_wizard
- **Brief description:** A guided tool (“wizard”) for uploading media files for use on Wikipedia and related Wikimedia projects.
- **On-page evidence (verbatim):** The Main Page’s extracted links include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:File_upload_wizard` (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).


## Notes on evidence gaps
I was able to verify (from the Main Page extraction) that these URLs are present as links on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page. However, the RAW VISIT CONTENT provided only includes the full text for the Main Page and a Special:WhatLinksHere page—not the full text of each of the 10 linked target pages. As a result, most of the 1–2 sentence descriptions above are high-level descriptions based on the well-known purpose implied by the page titles/paths, rather than being quoted from each target page’s content.

What I *can* quote directly for a description is the **Community portal** line from the Main Page itself: “**Community portal – The central hub for editors, with resources, links, tasks, and announcements.**” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).
final_output.action_summary: Collected 10 links shown in the extracted link list from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (Contents, Current events, Random article, About, Contact, Help, Learn to edit, Community portal, Recent changes, Upload file) and provided each URL + link title and a brief description. Only the Community portal description could be directly quoted from provided RAW VISIT CONTENT; the other target pages’ full texts were not included in the evidence bundle.
